channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Factory Activity Stalls in May as Demand Weakens
item.description: Chinas manufacturing sector showed signs of strain in May as factory activity flatlined, with new export orders contracting and input costs rising, raising concerns about the worlds second-largest economy.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israeli Forces Capture Strategic Castle in Southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli troops seized a Crusader-era mountain castle in southern Lebanon, marking their deepest incursion into the country in over 26 years amid ongoing cross-border tensions.

item.title: 🌏🛡️ US Allies Call for Unity at Singapore Defense Summit
item.description: American allies emphasized the need for global cooperation to address cross-regional security threats, even as the Trump administration criticizes traditional partners over burden-sharing.

item.title: 🇨🇩⚠️ WHO Chief Visits Epicenter of Ebola Outbreak in DR Congo
item.description: The WHO Director-General toured the heart of the Democratic Republic of Congos Ebola outbreak, warning that the spread is outpacing the global response and calling for more international funding.

item.title: 🇵🇭🇨🇳 Philippines Defense Chief Warns China Threat Persists Despite US-China Thaw
item.description: Manilas defense minister said the Philippines remains under severe threat from China despite the recent Trump-Xi summit that eased tensions between Washington and Beijing.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Planning Accelerated Troop Withdrawal from Europe
item.description: The Pentagon plans to speed up the withdrawal of US troops from European bases and will present proposals to NATO allies next month, according to German media citing an unidentified Pentagon source.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Foreign Minister to Visit China and India Amid Global Crises
item.description: British Foreign Minister Yvette Cooper will travel to China on Monday and then to India later in the week. The visits will focus on global issues including the Strait of Hormuz, the Russia-Ukraine war, and the Ebola outbreak.

item.title: 🇦🇹 Austrian Protesters Block Key Alpine Motorway Connecting Germany and Italy
item.description: Thousands of local residents shut down the Brenner motorway, a vital north-south corridor through the Alps, in protest against trucks and tourists clogging their roads.

item.title: 🇫🇷 SoftBank to Invest €45 Billion in AI Data Centers in France
item.description: Japan's SoftBank Group announced plans to invest €45 billion over five years to build artificial intelligence infrastructure in France, marking a major expansion of AI computing capacity in Europe.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Factory Activity Stalls in May as Demand Weakens
item.description: Chinas factory activity stalled in May as new export orders contracted and input costs kept rising, an official survey showed on Sunday, adding to concerns the worlds second-largest economy is losing momentum.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Claims It Shot Down US Military Drone Over Territorial Waters
item.description: Iran announced it shot down a US military drone over its territorial waters, escalating tensions amid ongoing negotiations between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Airstrike in Southern Lebanon Kills 9 Syrian Refugees
item.description: An Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon killed nine Syrian refugees from the same family, according to Anadolu Agency, marking a deadly escalation in cross-border hostilities.

item.title: 🇸🇾 Euphrates Flooding Displaces Thousands in Eastern Syria's Deir ez-Zor
item.description: Flooding along the Euphrates River has displaced thousands of people in eastern Syria's Deir ez-Zor province, adding to the humanitarian crisis in the war-torn region.

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australia to Receive Used US Submarines Under AUKUS Deal Adjustment
item.description: Australia will receive used US submarines under a modified AUKUS agreement, adjusting the original plan as Washington and Canberra work to strengthen Indo-Pacific naval capabilities.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Pushes Back Against Accusations of New Militarism
item.description: Japan defended its defense buildup plans against accusations of renewed militarism, as regional tensions with China and North Korea continue to shape Tokyo's security posture.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US Military Says It Turned Away Blockade Runner Trying to Reach Iranian Port
item.description: The US military announced it intercepted and turned away a vessel attempting to breach the blockade and reach an Iranian port, as part of ongoing enforcement operations in the Gulf.

item.title: 🌏 Where Is China? Delegates Ask at Asian Defense Forum
item.description: Delegates at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore questioned Chinas absence from key discussions, as Beijing skips the regions premier defense summit amid tensions with the US.

item.title: 🇻🇳 Vietnam Leader Says Better China Ties Can Help Regional Peace
item.description: Vietnams top leader said strong relations with China would benefit regional peace and security, while also emphasizing the importance of ties with the United States.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Drones Strike Port Oil Depot in Southern Russia
item.description: Ukrainian drones attacked a port oil depot in southern Russia, authorities said, as Kyiv continues to target Russian energy infrastructure in the ongoing war.

item.title: ☢️ Russian Rosatom Says Ukrainian Drone Struck Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant
item.description: Russias state nuclear agency Rosatom said a Ukrainian drone struck the Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant, raising safety concerns at Europes largest atomic facility.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Pentagon Chief Says US Ready to Restart Strikes on Iran If No Deal
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said the United States is prepared to resume attacks on Iran if negotiations fail to produce an agreement, speaking at a defense summit in Singapore.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Wait for Iran Deal Continues as Trump Makes Final Determination
item.description: The wait for a potential Iran nuclear deal continues as President Trump makes his final determination on whether to accept the proposed agreement with Tehran.

item.title: 🇫🇷 SoftBank Plans $88 Billion AI Data Hub in France as It Expands Beyond US
item.description: SoftBank Group plans to build an $88 billion AI data hub in France, marking the Japanese conglomerates major expansion of artificial intelligence infrastructure outside the United States.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Building Launch Pads Near Its Nuclear Missile Silos
item.description: Satellite imagery reveals China is constructing launch pads near its nuclear missile silos, raising concerns about Beijings strategic weapons capabilities and nuclear posture.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine War: Russia Launches Record Drone Attack on Kyiv
item.description: Russia launched a record drone attack on Kyiv overnight, Ukrainian officials said, in one of the largest aerial assaults on the capital since the war began.
